mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.66% as of 2017-02-15 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145,798 thousand as of 2017-02-15 indicates strong employment base
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 150,000, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) above 2% annualized growth rate signals strengthening consumer demand that translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when RSAFS 3-month growth > 2.0% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining more than 1.5% over three months signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO 3-month decline exceeds 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals synchronized demand weakness across residential and consumer sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to activate.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,246 thousand as of 2017-01-15, RSAFS at 473,991 million as of 2017-02-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.0% combined with month-over-month payroll decline signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE > 5.0% and PAYEMS declines, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.5% signals monetary policy lag that creates financial conditions supportive of equity valuations in the near term, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 244.456 as of 2017-02-15, FEDFUNDS at 0.66% as of 2017-02-15
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL 12-month growth > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured at quarterly frequency declining below 2.0% annualized rate signals sub-trend economic expansion that constrains corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism is always-on and does not require a trigger threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,804.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2016-10-15
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during sub-2.0% GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
